steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two TV programs. The notes say the slow TV genre consists of programs that document ordinary events in real time — that is the shared feature both programs must express.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A prime example of the slow TV genre can be seen in *All Aboard! The Sleigh Ride*, a 2-hour British TV documentary program documenting a sleigh ride along an arctic postal route.: mentions only one program. No comparison to Monsen is made. ✗
  • Choice B — While *All Aboard! The Sleigh Ride* depicts a sleigh ride along an arctic postal route, *Monsen: Minutt for Minutt* depicts a hike from Stigstuv to Rauhelleren in Norway.: mentions both programs but emphasizes their different subjects, not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice C — Compared to the 5-hour-long *Monsen: Minutt for Minutt*, the slow TV program *All Aboard! The Sleigh Ride* is relatively short, at 2 hours.: mentions both programs but emphasizes the difference in running time. ✗
  • Choice D — Documenting ordinary events in real time, *All Aboard! The Sleigh Ride* and *Monsen: Minutt for Minutt* are both documentary programs in the slow TV genre.: explicitly states the shared characteristic — both document ordinary events in real time and both belong to the slow TV genre. ✓
That matches Choice D — Documenting ordinary events in real time, *All Aboard! The Sleigh Ride* and *Monsen: Minutt for Minutt* are both documentary programs in the slow TV genre..
